summary: This brief post celebrates the intersection of knitting, science activism, and political resistance ahead of the March for Science. O'Neil highlights 'resistor hats' - knitted hats featuring the electronic resistor symbol - as a creative form of protest. She showcases variations including chemistry-themed, DNA helix, water molecule, and combination pussy hat/resistor designs, all available as free patterns on Ravelry. The post is essentially a celebration of nerdy craftivism.
key_points: Resistor hats combine knitting culture with science advocacy and political resistance
The knitting community has created multiple science-themed variations for the March for Science
Free patterns are available on Ravelry and craftivism sites, making participation accessible
The post highlights the creative energy of 'knitting nerd activists'
money_quotes: So, anyone surprised? I'm not. But I am excited.
Knitting nerd activists are endlessly creative
key_insight: Science advocates are finding creative ways to merge crafting culture with political resistance, creating accessible forms of protest.
